version: Senate Green Sheet Digest for HB108
fullText:
GREEN SHEET REDIGEST
HB 108 2026 Regular Session Cox
CRIMINAL/JURY TRIALS: Provides relative to juror qualifications.
DIGEST
Present law provides for the qualifications a person must meet to serve as a juror in a
criminal case.
Proposed law retains present law and adds that in order to serve as a juror in a criminal case,
a person must never have been convicted of a crime of violence or a sex offense.
Present law provides that the qualifications a person must meet to serve as a juror in a civil
case will be the same as for criminal cases.
Proposed law provides that the prohibition against a person convicted of a crime of violence
or a sex offense does not apply to jurors in a civil case.
(Amends R.S. 13:3041(B); adds C.Cr.P. Art. 401(A)(6))
Summary of Amendments Adopted by Senate
Committee Amendments Proposed by Senate Committee on Judiciary C to the
engrossed bill
1. Provide that a person convicted of certain offenses will not be automatically
prohibited from serving as a juror in a civil case.
